## 1. Our Commitment

Vantus Systems builds for your independence. This guide outlines the final phase where your organization takes full control over its digital assets.

**What Complete Ownership Means:**
- **Full Control:** You own every line of code, every configuration, every password
- **No Lock-In:** No hidden dependencies, no backdoors, no vendor traps
- **True Independence:** You can run, change, and grow the system without Vantus
- **Future Freedom:** You can hire any vendor or build your own team

### 1.1 The Ownership Promise

By the end of this transfer:
- You will have total control over your infrastructure
- Your team will be capable of independent operation
- All Vantus access will be removed (unless contractually agreed otherwise)
- You will have all knowledge necessary for self-sufficiency

---

## 2. Three Key Areas of Transfer

### 2.1 Intellectual Property Transfer

**What This Means:** Complete ownership and understanding of all intellectual property

**What You Receive:**
- All source code with full version history
- Complete documentation suite (85+ documents)
- Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
- Technical specifications
- Design assets and documentation
- Third-party licenses and compliance documentation

**Transfer Actions:**
1. **Code Repository Transfer**
   - [ ] All repositories copied to your Git platform
   - [ ] Complete Git history preserved
   - [ ] No external dependencies on Vantus infrastructure
   - [ ] All branches, tags, and releases transferred

2. **Documentation Handover**
   - [ ] `/docs/` folder delivered and indexed
   - [ ] All ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) included
   - [ ] Technical specifications complete
   - [ ] Runbooks tested and verified
   - [ ] FAQ current and complete

3. **Knowledge Verification**
   - [ ] Your team can explain architecture without help
   - [ ] Your team can navigate codebase independently
   - [ ] Your team can modify and extend functionality
   - [ ] Your team can troubleshoot common issues

**Success Check:** Your team demonstrates ability to explain system architecture to a third party without Vantus involvement.

---

### 2.2 Operational Transfer

**What This Means:** Complete capability to operate the system independently

**What You Receive:**
- All operational runbooks and procedures
- Monitoring and alerting configurations
- Incident response protocols
- Backup and disaster recovery procedures
- Maintenance schedules and checklists

**Transfer Actions:**
1. **Runbook Mastery**
   - [ ] All critical runbooks reviewed and understood
   - [ ] Your team executes procedures independently
   - [ ] Emergency procedures practiced and verified
   - [ ] Escalation paths documented and tested

2. **Tooling Access**
   - [ ] All monitoring dashboards accessible
   - [ ] All logging systems accessible
   - [ ] All administrative tools accessible
   - [ ] Alerting and notification configured for your team

3. **Operational Verification**
   - [ ] Your team performs deployment independently
   - [ ] Your team responds to simulated incident
   - [ ] Your team executes backup/restore procedure
   - [ ] Your team performs routine maintenance

**Success Check:** Your team completes "Independence Drill" — 48 hours of operation without Vantus contact.

---

### 2.3 Infrastructure Transfer

**What This Means:** Complete control over all infrastructure and hosting

**What You Receive:**
- Full ownership of all cloud accounts
- Control over all domains and DNS
- Management of all third-party services
- All credentials and passwords
- Exit strategy documentation

**Transfer Actions:**
1. **Account Ownership Transfer**
   - [ ] Root account ownership transferred to you
   - [ ] Billing responsibility transferred
   - [ ] User policies updated for your ownership
   - [ ] Vantus access keys removed
   - [ ] Two-factor authentication enabled on all root accounts

2. **Domain & Certificate Control**
   - [ ] Domain registrar account access transferred
   - [ ] DNS zone control verified
   - [ ] SSL certificate private keys handed over
   - [ ] Auto-renewal configured

3. **Third-Party Service Transfer**
   - [ ] All SaaS account ownership transferred
   - [ ] API keys regenerated
   - [ ] Billing updated to you
   - [ ] Vantus team members removed

4. **Exit Strategy Verification**
   - [ ] Ability to migrate providers documented
   - [ ] Migration procedures tested (theoretically)
   - [ ] No vendor lock-in dependencies identified
   - [ ] Data export procedures documented

**Success Check:** You can change any infrastructure provider without code changes or Vantus assistance.

---

## 3. Transfer Timeline

### 3.1 Pre-Transfer Phase (Weeks T-4 to T-2)

| Week | Activities | Deliverables | Owner |
|------|------------|--------------|-------|
| T-4 | Documentation audit | Gap analysis report | Vantus |
| T-4 | Access inventory | Complete access list | Joint |
| T-3 | Password cleanup | Clean credential inventory | Vantus |
| T-3 | Training delivery | Completed training sessions | Vantus |
| T-2 | Documentation completion | Final docs delivered | Vantus |
| T-2 | Access preparation | Transfer-ready accounts | Joint |

### 3.2 Transfer Phase (Week T-1 to T-0)

| Week | Activities | Deliverables | Owner |
|------|------------|--------------|-------|
| T-1 | Independence Drill | Drill completion report | Client |
| T-1 | Credential handover | Secure package delivered | Vantus |
| T-1 | Final review | Pre-transfer checklist | Joint |
| T-0 | Account transfers | Ownership confirmed | Joint |
| T-0 | Access removal | Vantus access removed | Vantus |
| T-0 | Final sign-off | Ownership certificate | Joint |

### 3.3 Post-Transfer Phase (Weeks T+1 to T+4)

| Week | Activities | Deliverables | Owner |
|------|------------|--------------|-------|
| T+1 to T+4 | Shadow Support | Observation-only support | Vantus |
| T+2 | First check-in | Status review | Joint |
| T+4 | Warranty review | Warranty status report | Vantus |

---

## 4. Detailed Transfer Procedures

### 4.1 Week T-2: Documentation Audit & Password Cleanup

**Documentation Audit:**
```
1. Review all /docs/ for completeness
2. Verify all placeholders replaced with actual values
3. Check all links and references
4. Validate all procedures with dry runs
5. Update version numbers and dates
6. Create document index
```

**Password Cleanup:**
```
1. List all credentials and passwords
2. Identify Vantus-specific credentials
3. Generate new credentials for your ownership
4. Document rotation schedule
5. Prepare secure package
6. Verify no Vantus emails in account recovery
```

**Deliverables:**
- [ ] Documentation completeness report
- [ ] Credential inventory (clean)
- [ ] Secure package prepared

---

### 4.2 Week T-1: The Independence Drill

**Purpose:** Verify your team can operate without Vantus

**Drill Components:**

**Component 1: Cold Start (4 hours)**
```
Scenario: Complete system rebuild from scratch
Your Actions:
- Clone repositories
- Set up infrastructure
- Deploy application
- Verify functionality

Success Criteria:
- System operational within 4 hours
- No Vantus assistance requested
- All tests passing
```

**Component 2: Incident Response (2 hours)**
```
Scenario: Simulated critical incident
Your Actions:
- Detect incident through monitoring
- Execute incident response runbook
- Resolve the issue
- Document post-incident report

Success Criteria:
- Incident resolved within 2 hours
- Proper communication sent
- Root cause identified
```

**Component 3: Feature Deployment (2 hours)**
```
Scenario: Deploy new feature end-to-end
Your Actions:
- Develop feature locally
- Deploy to staging
- Test thoroughly
- Deploy to production
- Verify in production

Success Criteria:
- Feature live in production
- No regressions
- Deployment logged
```

**Drill Evaluation:**
| Component | Time | Result | Notes |
|-----------|------|--------|-------|
| Cold Start | [[TIME]] | [ ] Pass / [ ] Fail | [[NOTES]] |
| Incident Response | [[TIME]] | [ ] Pass / [ ] Fail | [[NOTES]] |
| Feature Deployment | [[TIME]] | [ ] Pass / [ ] Fail | [[NOTES]] |

**Overall Result:** [ ] Drill Passed / [ ] Remediation Required

---

### 4.3 Week T-0: Mirror Repositories & Remove Access

**Repository Mirroring:**
```
1. Final sync of all repositories
2. Verify you have admin access
3. Transfer repository ownership (if applicable)
4. Update webhook URLs to your systems
5. Verify CI/CD pipelines functional
```

**Access Removal:**
```
1. Remove Vantus SSH keys from all servers
2. Remove Vantus team from all accounts
3. Revoke Vantus API keys
4. Update password manager entries
5. Verify no Vantus access remains
6. Document any retained access (if contractually agreed)
```

**Final Verification:**
```
1. You confirm full access
2. Vantus confirms no remaining access
3. Third-party services verified
4. Billing responsibility confirmed
5. Support contacts updated
```

---

### 4.4 Post-T: 30-Day "Shadow Support" Window

**Shadow Support Definition:**
- Vantus observes only — no intervention unless requested
- Your team leads all operations
- Vantus available for consultation (office hours)
- Emergency support for critical issues (warranty)

**Shadow Support Activities:**
| Week | Activity | Purpose |
|------|----------|---------|
| T+1 | Observation | Monitor your operations |
| T+2 | Check-in call | Address any questions |
| T+3 | Documentation | Update FAQ with new questions |
| T+4 | Final review | Warranty status and closure |

**Support Boundaries:**
- ✓ Answer questions
- ✓ Clarify documentation
- ✓ Emergency incident support
- ✗ Perform operations
- ✗ Make configuration changes
- ✗ Access production systems

---

## 5. Credential Escrow & Emergency Access

### 5.1 Escrow Package Contents

| Item | Description | Location | Access Method |
|------|-------------|----------|---------------|
| Master Password List | All admin passwords | Encrypted USB | Physical safe |
| API Keys | Production API credentials | Password Manager | MFA + master password |
| SSL Private Keys | Certificate private keys | HSM/Secure Storage | Dual control |
| Database Credentials | Root database access | Vault | Emergency procedure |
| Cloud Root Access | Root account recovery | Secure Envelope | Executive access |

### 5.2 Emergency Access Procedure

**When to Use:**
- All primary administrators unavailable
- Critical system failure requiring root access
- Security incident requiring immediate containment

**Procedure:**
```
1. Incident Commander declares emergency
2. Two authorized personnel retrieve escrow
3. Access credentials using dual-control
4. Document all actions taken
5. Rotate all accessed credentials within 24 hours
6. Conduct post-incident review
```

**Authorized Personnel:**
- [[NAME]] - [[TITLE]]
- [[NAME]] - [[TITLE]]
- [[NAME]] - [[TITLE]]

**Escrow Custodian:** [[CUSTODIAN_NAME]]  
**Escrow Location:** [[ESCROW_LOCATION]]

## 7. Emergency Re-Engagement

### 7.1 Re-engagement Policy

Should you wish to re-hire Vantus for a future sprint:

**Requirements:**
- New Statement of Work (SOW) required
- Current system state assessment
- Scope definition and timeline
- Cost estimation and approval

**Process:**
1. Contact Vantus: [[BUSINESS_CONTACT]]
2. Describe requirements and timeline
3. Vantus conducts discovery
4. Proposal delivered
5. Contract negotiation
6. Engagement begins

### 7.2 No Persistent Access

**Vantus Commitment:**
- We leave no "backdoor" access
- We maintain no persistent credentials
- Your system is closed to us post-transfer
- Any future access requires explicit authorization

**This Means:**
- You have complete privacy
- No surprise access
- Full control over when/if we return
- True independence maintained
